writer.name: 森格多吉·占齐夫多吉 Sengedorj Janchivdorj

director.name: 森格多吉·占齐夫多吉 Sengedorj Janchivdorj

introduction: Bayaraa, a weather-worn muralist who spent his glory years in Europe, has drifted back to Ulaanbaatar with nothing but a rickety rooftop tent, a box of fading paints, and Dolingor, the stray dog that shadows his every step. Each dawn he rappels down an abandoned Soviet-era factory wall, splashing mythic Mongolian landscapes across crumbling concrete while a sardonic red balloon—equal parts conscience and comic Greek chorus—floats overhead.When city officials announce the site will be bulldozed for a turkey farm, Bayaraa digs in, certain this mural will be his life's masterpiece. His only allies are a curious herder boy who's never owned a crayon, and Kosoy, a Japanese photojournalist whose camera captures the attention of fellow art tourists from Japan. While Bayaraa faces pressure from bureaucrats and developers, the deeper fight lies within: decades-old guilt over the wife and daughter he abandoned for art.Drawing on surreal realism and long, breathing takes that keep us at the artist's elbow, director J. Sengedorj weaves Mongolia's gritty urban present with its shamanic inner world. The Muralist becomes a hymn to imagination's stubborn survival—whether in spray-painted saints, a boy's first charcoal line, or the quiet forgiveness that can still bloom on a ruined wall.
